differential_diagnoses: Streptococcus anginosus group liver abscess/duodenal penetration
next_steps: Perform transthoracic or transesophageal echocardiography to rule out endocarditis
Perform upper endoscopy to evaluate duodenal wall thickening and possible perforation/fistula
Obtain follow-up imaging (repeat MRI or CT) of the abdomen to better characterize the duodenal-renal interface
Continue targeted antibiotic therapy for Streptococcus anginosus group
Consult interventional radiology for potential drainage of suspected retroperitoneal collection
Continue anticoagulation therapy with close monitoring of thrombotic progression
Evaluate for secondary causes of thrombocytopenia and coagulation abnormalities

differential_diagnoses: Penetrating duodenal ulcer with secondary retroperitoneal abscess and septic thrombophlebitis
Streptococcus anginosus group bacteremia with focal abscesses
Catastrophic antiphospholipid syndrome (CAPS) triggered by infection
Strongyloides hyperinfection syndrome with bacterial translocation
Systemic lupus erythematosus with associated APS and vasculitis
next_steps: Perform transthoracic echocardiography to rule out endocarditis
Order Strongyloides IgG serology and stool examination for parasites
Obtain high-quality, motion-corrected abdominal MRI or CT to evaluate the duodenal-renal fistula interface
Perform upper endoscopy with caution to evaluate for ulceration or fistula
Obtain repeat antiphospholipid panel (B2GP1, anticardiolipin) in 12 weeks to confirm persistent APS
Consult interventional radiology for potential drainage of the retroperitoneal collection
Continue systemic anticoagulation and monitor for signs of hemorrhage
